127663.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA finds it important that residents can have a say. Therefore, the party wants a referendum on major decisions. When residents have chosen, the municipal council can make better choices supported by many residents.

127666.explanation: If cafés and restaurants can choose their closing times, residents nearby suffer too much at night. Those living in the center must tolerate some noise, but we want this nuisance not to become too great.

127668.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA has no agreement to stop refugee reception in 2028 and does not want that. Refugees deserve proper shelter. We believe Zeist should continue this as long as needed. We ourselves would want shelter if we had to flee.

127669.explanation: Wood stoves cause dirty air. People with lung diseases suffer a lot from this. For now, we still want to allow wood stoves. First, we want to inform residents when they should better not burn and how to burn as cleanly as possible. If that does not help, we want to ban it.

127670.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA finds terrace heating unnecessary and a waste of energy. If it's cold, you can sit inside. We don't want to ban it immediately. First, we want to make agreements with cafés and restaurants to stop heating terraces. If that doesn't help, we want to ban it.

127673.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA has not received complaints about noise nuisance from festivals and parties. Festivals already have to meet many requirements. It must remain possible to organize a festival.

127674.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA finds it important that everyone can properly use buildings. Therefore, entrepreneurs should be able to get subsidies to improve their buildings. Such a subsidy already exists: the Stimulus Subsidy for Building Accessibility. It helps entrepreneurs make their buildings better accessible.

127676.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants all children in Zeist to have equal opportunities to learn and grow. Music lessons are part of that. Currently, all children in primary school receive music lessons. Parents who cannot afford after-school music lessons can receive funding through the cultural fund to make it possible.

127680.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes you should think carefully about where to place cameras. Research shows cameras do not always make an area safer. Cameras can also be bad for residents' privacy.

127682.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes only the police may use force, and only in emergencies.

127683.explanation: Social rental homes can already be given to a small percentage of applicants from the own municipality. GroenLinks-PvdA does not want to increase this percentage because then other housing seekers from the region will have no chance to live in the municipality of Zeist. No fence around Zeist!

127685.explanation: When you buy a home, the intention is to live in it yourself. With a special rule called purchase protection, we ensure homes go to people who will live in them themselves. This prevents people from buying houses to make money.

127687.explanation: Temporary housing for students can help reduce the housing shortage quickly. But in addition, we must continue to ensure good permanent housing.

127688.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA opposes mandatory work to receive welfare benefits. However, the party wants to encourage people to do work that benefits society. We also want to make the rules more flexible so people can gain work experience without immediately losing their benefits.

127689.explanation: Too many people in Zeist live in poverty. They have such a low income that they cannot pay municipal taxes. The municipality cannot change people's income but can help by waiving these taxes. This way they are less likely to get into debt.

127694.explanation: Because you have to pay for parking in the center, some people who come shopping look for a free parking spot nearby. This causes problems in neighborhoods close to the center. Parking by permit only can help solve this problem.

127695.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA thinks this is a good idea and wants to explore whether an environmental zone is possible. Without enforcement, such a zone does not work well. Therefore, drivers who enter the environmental zone with a polluting car should also receive a fine.

127696.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes that healthcare providers should be friendly and professional, with respect for everyone's beliefs. People should not choose care based on their religion. That makes mutual understanding and living together more difficult. Moreover, it makes healthcare much too expensive.

127698.explanation: This is a tax for homeowners. In Zeist, it is one of the lowest in the Netherlands. It is one of the few taxes the municipality decides on itself. Sometimes the property tax (ozb) must be increased to pay for improvements. A 10 percent increase means 5 euros per month for a home worth one million.

127702.explanation: Longer opening hours make it harder for local shopkeepers. They often do not want this. Shopkeepers then have to work longer days or hire extra staff. This usually does not result in enough extra sales.

127703.explanation: Refugees with a residence permit already get priority for a social rental home, just like Dutch people in urgent need. Figures show that this concerns about seven out of every one hundred social rental homes.

127706.explanation: Every child has the right to learn to swim in this country with lots of water. GroenLinks-PvdA believes school swimming lessons are the best way to teach as many children as possible to swim.

127707.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to allow soft drugs. Otherwise, users come into contact with illegal trade more quickly. That is why the party is also not against opening coffee shops. Currently, we believe there is no application for a coffee shop.

127709.explanation: To protect nature and reduce the housing shortage, it is necessary to build higher in some places than we have done so far.

127710.explanation: This especially applies to Den Dolder, which currently does not yet have a village center.

127712.explanation: A number of streets are already car-free. The shopping area becomes more attractive if we expand this, for example with Slotlaan and the Market. With parking garages around the center, the center remains easily accessible. For people with physical disabilities, parking in the center must remain possible.

127713.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA finds it important that the KNVB stays in Zeist. But plans to expand must not damage nature. We expect to be able to make good agreements with the KNVB about this.

127714.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes that Fivoor must do what has been agreed and leave before January 1, 2027. But Zeist must also ensure good forensic care in the region. It is difficult to find another suitable location in Zeist, so we prefer a location in the Zeist area.

127716.explanation: Not car-free, but car-light. Residents are encouraged to travel as much as possible by public transport, bicycle, or shared car. Therefore, fewer parking spaces are provided than in other new developments. Residents with disabilities do have space for their own car.

127717.explanation: The wolf is good for nature but not welcome everywhere. That is why we want clear rules on how to deal with the wolf. Attention must be paid to both the safety of residents and the habitat of the wolf.

127663.explanation: The VVD supports residents having influence on important decisions. A great example is the Citizen's Assembly, which we have had good experiences with in Zeist. We see no added value in a referendum, but do see the necessary disadvantages.

127666.explanation: Entrepreneurs should be able to make choices themselves wherever possible. Only where necessary - such as closing times of nightclubs - can the municipality impose conditions.

127668.explanation: For the VVD, agreements stand once the municipality has committed to them. Only in this way can the government be reliable.

127669.explanation: The VVD has confidence in the willingness of residents with a fireplace or wood stove to consider their neighbors.

127670.explanation: The VVD believes entrepreneurs should have room to do business. That means we are cautious about banning things. However, we do see opportunities to support entrepreneurs in becoming more sustainable.

127673.explanation: Noise standards do not need to be stricter everywhere. It is important to make good agreements about this, so organizers know how much noise is allowed at a particular location. The municipality can then enforce this effectively.

127674.explanation: An entrepreneur is primarily responsible for making their business accessible. The municipality has a limited role and can help by communicating the importance of this, and a subsidy scheme is also conceivable.

127676.explanation: The municipality should only intervene where it is really necessary. This form of subsidy already exists in Zeist but is focused.

127680.explanation: Camera surveillance demonstrably leads to greater safety because it makes it easier to track down offenders. Cameras also increase the sense of security. As far as we are concerned, we place cameras mainly where nuisance is experienced and where residents request them.

127682.explanation: We believe that the public order officers (boa's) in Zeist should be equipped as well as possible. Recently they have received bodycams, and a baton is certainly conceivable according to the VVD.

127683.explanation: We believe that people born and raised in Zeist deserve a home here. The same applies to people who work in Zeist. Wherever possible, we adjust housing allocation regionally so we can steer this better.

127685.explanation: It is important that more homes are built in Zeist, which means we encourage people to invest in homes, also if these are rented out. We also do not want to forbid parents from buying a home for their own child.

127687.explanation: Space in Zeist is scarce, so it is important to make good choices. In this respect, we are not in favor of temporary and non-sustainable solutions such as container homes and tiny houses.

127688.explanation: In principle, a counter-performance is required if you are temporarily dependent on social assistance. With a strong preference, we guide people on social assistance to work as soon as possible, also because there are currently many jobs available.

127689.explanation: This form of debt relief causes the step to paid work to be bigger because people gain too little by exchanging benefits for a paid job.

127694.explanation: We gladly leave this choice to the resident. We consider this per street and only do this if the majority of residents request it.

127695.explanation: We are not in favor of this. We find it important that the center remains accessible to everyone, also because important social facilities are located here.

127696.explanation: The VVD supports neutrality, also regarding providers of care facilities. The VVD puts the quality of care central and not religion or culture.

127698.explanation: The VVD stands for the lowest possible taxes. We are known for this in Zeist, and we want to keep it that way. Where necessary, we prefer cutting spending over raising taxes.

127702.explanation: Entrepreneurs need space to do business. We do not see why shopkeepers should not be allowed to open on Sunday mornings. Moreover, shops in neighboring municipalities partly do open on Sunday mornings, causing our shopkeepers to lose sales.

127703.explanation: We operate on the principle of equal treatment, which means we distribute the scarce supply of social rental homes as fairly as possible. Of course, special urgent cases can be prioritized within this category.

127706.explanation: The municipality should only intervene where it is really necessary. If we provide subsidies, we do so as targeted as possible.

127707.explanation: We see no reason for that whatsoever.

127709.explanation: There is a great need for housing. At the same time, we want to preserve the character of Zeist. This means that we do not exclude high-rise buildings but only want them in locations where it is possible, such as near Driebergen-Zeist station.

127710.explanation: We consider a village hall (and similarly neighborhood centers) very important for connection between residents. Where residents value this, we realize a village hall or neighborhood center.

127712.explanation: This was attempted a few years ago and was very unpopular because people had to make significant detours. Furthermore, we consider it very important that the center remains easily accessible by car, both for residents and visitors.

127713.explanation: The KNVB is an important showcase for Zeist and provides employment, so we especially want to keep them in Zeist. The current coalition is also strongly committed to this.

127714.explanation: We believe that Fivoor should establish itself in another municipality.

127716.explanation: From the principle of freedom of choice, we believe that people in this neighborhood should also have the option to choose to have a car.

127717.explanation: The safety of people and pets is our top priority. At the same time, we are dealing with the wolf, which means we focus on a clear and active approach.

127663.explanation: To involve Zeist residents more in local government and increase their say, D66 wants to experiment with new forms of local democracy. Every resident can contribute to numerous new forms of administrative and democratic innovation. A referendum can be one such tool.

127666.explanation: D66 supports giving entrepreneurs and shopkeepers the freedom to choose their own opening hours. For nightlife venues, hospitality permits and operating licenses remain in effect.

127668.explanation: We take responsibility for refugees staying in Zeist and, in close consultation with the community, will establish (temporary) housing and facilities for them. We remain willing to help them. D66 especially pays attention to the vulnerable position of asylum-seeking children.

127669.explanation: D66 focuses on a healthy living environment and reducing emissions/particulate matter. D66 wants less wood burning (no wood burning in new construction) and better information on wood burning during unfavorable weather. D66 is not in favor of a local ban in Zeist.

127670.explanation: D66 Zeist is not in favor of a local ban on terrace heating. D66 aims for a healthy living environment and wants to reduce emissions. Alternatives such as blankets and electric heating should be preferred.

127673.explanation: Zeist has wonderful events such as the Zeister Music Days, International Song Festival, and many more. D66 welcomes this and sees room for more festivals and open-air events for broad audiences. At the same time, we are mindful of the impact on neighborhoods and nature.

127674.explanation: People with disabilities, the elderly, or the chronically ill must be able to fully participate in society. That starts with an accessible public space, a safe street, and (wheelchair) accessible sidewalks. D66 wants to maintain the accessibility incentive subsidy for buildings.

127676.explanation: Sports and culture contribute to connection, inspiration, and enjoyment. D66 considers it important that sports and culture are accessible to all residents of the municipality. The youth sports and culture fund must be preserved.

127680.explanation: Camera surveillance only when truly necessary. D66 opposes permanent camera surveillance and is cautious about using temporary camera surveillance and preventive searches. These measures must always be temporary, targeted, and proportionate.

127682.explanation: There is good cooperation between the police and public order officers (boa's) in Zeist. Currently, there is no need or desire to deploy additional weapons beyond what the police already have.

127683.explanation: The housing market is a regional market. The vast majority of available social rental homes are already allocated to residents of Zeist.

127685.explanation: Newly built homes already have an owner-occupancy obligation (purchase protection). D66 wants to maintain this owner-occupancy obligation.

127687.explanation: More space should be created to temporarily house urgent seekers. Students can be one of these groups. At the same time, we advocate that housing projects also provide room for structural student housing.

127688.explanation: D66 advocates that municipalities be given more freedom to experiment with social assistance programs that encourage people to develop themselves and work. D66 also wants it to be more attractive to accept a temporary or part-time job from social assistance.

127689.explanation: D66 wants to prevent people from falling into debt and, if it happens, to ensure they get out of debt as quickly as possible. The municipality must contribute in various ways to preventing and resolving payment problems.

127694.explanation: Only if necessary and with sufficient support from the neighborhood itself.

127695.explanation: There is no necessity for this at the moment.

127696.explanation: D66 chooses appropriate care and good quality. Personal preferences can play a role but are not always decisive. Due to increasing pressure on healthcare, we cannot always guarantee this.

127698.explanation: The property tax (ozb) in Zeist is relatively low compared to the national average. D66 is not in favor of a further reduction.

127702.explanation: D66 supports giving entrepreneurs and shopkeepers the freedom to choose their own opening hours, including on Sundays.

127703.explanation: Refugees with residence permits are one of the groups given priority within the housing regulations in Zeist. In 2024, this concerned 7 percent of the available social rental homes. This priority scheme must be maintained.

127706.explanation: If children cannot obtain a diploma, we must work with schools to focus on swimming safety. We see that certain groups and neighborhoods need extra attention for this. The youth sports and culture fund can be used for this.

127707.explanation: D66 Zeist takes a neutral stance on this. There is currently one coffeeshop, and if an application comes for a second, it will be assessed like any other permit.

127709.explanation: D66 wants to continue working on the housing shortage in Zeist, and higher (new) construction and adding extra floors could help. In neighborhoods like Kerckebosch, it has been demonstrated that this is possible in a very beautiful, nature-inclusive way.

127710.explanation: D66 finds it important that residents can meet each other and advocates for maintaining a high-quality network of neighborhood meeting places and community centers in our village cores and neighborhoods.

127712.explanation: Making the center completely car-free is not possible. However, D66 supports a low-car center and a low-car Slotlaan.

127713.explanation: The KNVB is part of Zeist, and we want to keep it that way. D66 will strive to retain the KNVB within Zeist. However, the KNVB must comply with nature permits like other organizations.

127714.explanation: The forensic clinic Fivoor has indicated it will leave the municipality of Zeist. D66 will hold Fivoor to this promise.

127716.explanation: The new district near Driebergen-Zeist is located at a mobility hub with many different public transport options. Additionally, there is space to park cars in parking garages around the station. The neighborhood itself will be low-car.

127717.explanation: Primarily, the province is responsible for providing resources to protect people and animals. As a municipality, we can support entrepreneurs with this. Simultaneously, we focus on education and coexistence with the wolf.

127663.explanation: Referendums reduce complex decisions to a yes-or-no choice, which does not do justice to the complexity of these proposals. Referendums increase and sharpen divisions within society instead of producing a solution that satisfies many people.

127666.explanation: As far as CDA Zeist is concerned, the current opening hours are sufficient, and exceptions can be made upon request.

127668.explanation: CDA Zeist supports maintaining the long-standing tradition that Zeist has of properly and carefully sheltering people fleeing danger. We believe it is important that we as a society are ready to help people in need, if necessary also after 2028, preferably by retaining current locations.

127669.explanation: CDA Zeist advocates encouraging residents rather than prohibiting. Good information could help reduce wood burning.

127670.explanation: CDA Zeist advocates encouraging residents rather than prohibiting. Good information could reduce the use of terrace heating.

127673.explanation: The current standards meet legal requirements. Therefore, CDA Zeist sees no reason to adjust them.

127674.explanation: CDA Zeist values accessibility, but the municipality of Zeist does not always need to pay for these adjustments. We assess each situation together with the entrepreneur(s) to determine what is needed and feasible.

127676.explanation: CDA Zeist supports music and culture. However, their funding does not necessarily have to come from the municipality. For residents who cannot afford it, funding is available through a minimum support scheme.

127680.explanation: CDA Zeist is not fundamentally opposed to camera surveillance, but it should only be deployed when necessary. Expansion is currently not desired by CDA Zeist. Temporary placement of cameras where desired is preferred.

127682.explanation: CDA Zeist believes that enforcement officers should be well protected and may be given more resources to perform their work safely, including a baton if needed. If the municipal executive calls for this, CDA Zeist will cooperate.

127683.explanation: When allocating social rental housing, CDA Zeist wants residents with a clear connection to Zeist to be given priority. This ensures people can stay in their own neighborhood and community remains strong. Given the regional housing market, this will be limited.

127685.explanation: Not everyone who buys a home can or wants to live in it themselves. A general requirement limits freedom of choice and can hinder investment and housing flow. We prefer targeted measures against abuses, not a strict rule for everyone.

127687.explanation: CDA Zeist believes this is not the primary task of the municipality of Zeist.

127688.explanation: Those receiving social assistance benefits who are able to do so should contribute socially. This helps people stay active and increases their chances of finding work. It involves suitable and meaningful work, with attention to health and personal circumstances. Naturally, this requires customization.

127689.explanation: Exemption from municipal taxes is important for residents with low incomes. This prevents people from falling further into trouble and keeps basic services affordable for everyone.

127694.explanation: Neighborhood residents themselves know best what is needed. Parking policies should be determined together with the neighborhood and not imposed from above. We choose customized approaches per neighborhood with support from residents.

127695.explanation: A low-emission zone excludes residents and entrepreneurs and affects people without alternatives. We opt for cleaner mobility through encouragement and customization, not by imposing a strict measure on the city center.

127696.explanation: Everyone must receive fast and good care. That is paramount. Choice based on religion or beliefs is important but must never lead to longer waiting times or reduced access. Good care for all is the first and foremost priority.

127698.explanation: Zeist already has some of the lowest property tax rates in the region. Further reductions are unnecessary and would put services under pressure. We opt for stable charges and good services for all residents.

127702.explanation: CDA Zeist does not support extending Sunday shopping hours. A 24-hour economy is not good for a healthy balance in our society. Moreover, this protects the position of our local entrepreneurs.

127703.explanation: CDA Zeist believes housing allocation should be based on connection to the municipality and urgency, not only on specific groups. Refugees with residence permits can be considered but do not receive automatic priority. As a principle, we follow national policy.

127706.explanation: The municipality supports school swimming for children who cannot afford it through minimum income policies. This helps children in need. We choose a targeted approach so funds reach families requiring support.

127707.explanation: The current coffeeshop policy meets the demand. An extra coffeeshop is unnecessary. We oppose drug use and want to prevent nuisance and crime. The municipality should maintain the existing policy.

127709.explanation: Zeist has a large housing shortage. By building higher in suitable locations, we create more homes without using additional space. This provides more opportunities for starters, families, and elderly people to find suitable housing.

127710.explanation: Not every district can sustain a full (center) hub. We choose realism. However, we support village halls and meeting places where residents gather. This keeps social cohesion strong, appropriate to the size of each Zeist district.

127712.explanation: CDA Zeist opposes a car-free city center. We support a car-reduced center with space for residents, entrepreneurs, and visitors. With good signage, we redirect through traffic around the center.

127713.explanation: CDA Zeist wants to keep the KNVB in Zeist. Zeist is the center circle of the Netherlands.

127714.explanation: CDA Zeist expects Fivoor to have left Den Dolder by January 1, 2027, as previously promised. According to CDA Zeist, relocating to another location in Zeist is not desirable!

127716.explanation: Plans for the new district near Driebergen-Zeist station are still under development. We want to first await the detailed plan and assess reachability, livability, and feasibility before taking a final position.

127717.explanation: For CDA Zeist, the safety of people and animals comes first. Together with the province and region, a targeted approach must be developed. The regulated removal of wolves is open for discussion with CDA Zeist.

127663.explanation: Zeist Lokaal wants maximum participation so residents not only think along but also decide on important local choices.

127666.explanation: Local entrepreneurs often know their customers and neighborhood better than the municipality. The municipality should only intervene in opening hours if complaints come from the neighborhood.

127668.explanation: Local agreements and support are important; if agreements have been made with our residents about closing in 2028, Zeist Lokaal supports respecting these.

127669.explanation: Zeist Lokaal respects tradition and the living environment. We support regulation where necessary but oppose patronizing bans that limit residents.

127670.explanation: A complete ban goes too far. We prefer to seek sustainable alternatives together without unnecessarily hindering hospitality businesses.

127673.explanation: Zeist Lokaal seeks balance between conviviality and livability; strict standards protect local residents without making events impossible.

127674.explanation: Everyone should be able to participate! Accessibility is an investment in a municipality where everyone has a voice.

127676.explanation: Culture and music enrich education; subsidies make it accessible to all children, not just a few.

127680.explanation: Camera surveillance can reduce nuisance and crime if privacy is carefully considered: only where truly needed!

127682.explanation: Enforcement must be carried out safely and responsibly. We believe that a baton enhances safety.

127683.explanation: Residents who already live in Zeist, have a social network here, or work here should not have to wait at the back of their own housing market.

127685.explanation: This prevents speculation and keeps homes available for residents instead of investors.

127687.explanation: Students are struggling in the current housing market. Zeist Lokaal wants to accommodate them.

127688.explanation: Participation where possible increases self-esteem and involvement, provided it is appropriate and not mandatory in vulnerable situations.

127689.explanation: Currently, tax exemptions are funded by the residents themselves. Zeist Lokaal believes these costs should be borne by the municipality.

127694.explanation: Zeist Lokaal sees no added value in expanding parking permits beyond the city center.

127695.explanation: Cleaner air is important, but an environmental zone in the center mainly affects entrepreneurs and visitors. We prefer to encourage cleaner transportation rather than impose a ban that harms the local economy.

127696.explanation: Good care must be available to everyone, regardless of background. The focus should be on professional quality and accessibility, not on organizing care along cultural or religious lines.

127698.explanation: Housing costs are already high for our residents. The municipality should not burden residents with extra costs if it is not necessary.

127702.explanation: Entrepreneurs and customers should be able to choose for themselves. The municipality should provide space, not impose obligations.

127703.explanation: We wish status holders a safe and good start, but housing scarcity is severe for everyone. Priority for one group feels unfair to many residents and undermines support. We must distribute fairly, considering wait times and local ties.

127706.explanation: Swimming skills are a basic safety requirement for every child. Learning to swim should not depend on the income of parents. Therefore, the municipality should take responsibility in this matter.

127707.explanation: There is no clear demand from residents for an additional coffee shop. The municipality should not create facilities that are not needed and that may cause extra nuisance.

127709.explanation: We want to build homes, but not at the cost of the green and village-like character of Zeist. High-rise buildings often do not fit well and can harm livability and the streetscape. We prefer small-scale, well-integrated construction focused on Zeist residents.

127710.explanation: A village center is often the heart of the community. It strengthens meeting, involvement, and the feeling of living together, especially in smaller communities.

127712.explanation: Less traffic and more space for pedestrians is a good goal. But a completely car-free center makes it difficult to reach for some people, such as the elderly and entrepreneurs. Therefore, we do not choose a car-free center.

127713.explanation: The KNVB is important for the recognition, employment, and image of Zeist. The organization belongs to the municipality and adds clear value without having to come at the expense of (much) nature.

127714.explanation: We believe Fivoor must close at this location and be given an appropriate place outside the municipality. The clinic no longer fits in a residential area like Den Dolder and the municipality of Zeist.

127716.explanation: A car-reduced neighborhood near a public transport hub is logical and future-oriented. This means less space is needed for roads and parking, making room for greenery and meeting places.

127717.explanation: Entrepreneurs and families living or walking in rural areas must not suffer from the wolf. Therefore, extra protection is needed and an active dialogue with the province of Utrecht about responsibility and approach.

127663.explanation: We have our Zeist participation opportunities. Through this method, residents can share their opinions.

127666.explanation: Opening hours impact employees and the environment. Therefore, these interests must be considered, and it is good that the municipality has control.

127668.explanation: All options must be investigated, including extending the current location’s opening hours. But ChristenUnie-SGP is open to starting small-scale locations as pilots. The concerns about asylum seeker reception are understandable but often turn out to be less in practice. More pilots, then.

127669.explanation: We want clean air. Therefore, we propose research at the neighborhood level to enable a targeted approach. We appeal to residents to follow the heating guide.

127670.explanation: Engage in dialogue with entrepreneurs and come to more sustainable options, for example with My Green Business. Awareness among residents is also important.

127673.explanation: No complaints about major noise disturbances are known to us. Therefore, no stricter rules. If you choose to live near a festival area, you are also aware of Zeist’s liveliness.

127674.explanation: Entrepreneurs do everything to be customer-friendly. This includes welcoming people with disabilities. The municipality can offer support.

127676.explanation: Zeist is affiliated with the Youth Culture/Sports Fund, which supports low-income families with these kinds of costs. This way, funds reach families who really need them, and all children can take music lessons.

127680.explanation: Not if we have no signals that it is necessary.

127682.explanation: Not if there are no indications that they need it.

127683.explanation: We have arranged this in the housing ordinance up to a maximum of 50 percent. Furthermore, it appears that residents from Zeist also often look for housing outside Zeist. If all municipalities build only for their own residents, we solve nothing.

127685.explanation: For a certain house value up to 728,000 euros, we have already arranged purchase protection in Zeist. Above this threshold, this rule should not apply.

127687.explanation: The municipality can steer but should leave the housing market as much as possible to developers and the like. Temporary housing could be deployed for a broad target group.

127688.explanation: We find it important that the RDWI stays in contact with them to explore possibilities. It would be great if these residents stay among people. But 'must' is too strong.

127689.explanation: For this group, the burden is too high. We have enough residents in Zeist who can bear a bit more.

127694.explanation: As little as possible. Only investigations if residents ask for it or because parking is causing problems.

127695.explanation: Our center is too small to impose specific requirements.

127696.explanation: Where possible. We also ask adaptations from our residents. Contracts with all providers become unaffordable. We also want care of high quality.

127698.explanation: It is not a goal of ChristenUnie-SGP to have a lower property tax. In Zeist, the rate is already low.

127702.explanation: Stores can be open 90 hours per week. To lighten the burden on small entrepreneurs and employees, we could have fewer opening hours. Consumers can only spend their money once. Peace and relaxation are of great value.

127703.explanation: From the national government, we receive tasks to house a set number of refugees annually. We want to fulfill that assignment.

127706.explanation: Just like with music lessons, parents can generally pay this themselves. For parents with an income at social assistance level, there are schemes that provide compensation for swimming lesson costs. The Sports and Culture Fund.

127707.explanation: We want to prohibit drugs and gambling. Harmful to people and their environment.

127709.explanation: To protect nature and still provide new homes, building upwards is necessary. Buildings should fit in the environment. The question is also what we consider 'high'.

127710.explanation: It is important that villages and neighborhoods have facilities nearby.

127712.explanation: It would be an ideal picture. But many entrepreneurs ask for a car-restricted center. The center is extensive and not ideal to visit mainly on foot. The bicycle but also the car still play a role.

127713.explanation: This depends on the overall plan. The aim is to keep it in Zeist, but not at all costs. Therefore, we first want to see the entire plan. In addition, the KNVB still has to compensate green spaces from the past. A reliable partner must keep its agreements.

127714.explanation: They must leave Den Dolder. That has been agreed upon and building plans have already been established because of this. In principle, the new location could be within our municipal boundaries if all conditions can be met. The chance of that is small. We must keep an eye on good care.

127716.explanation: Car-restricted. Cars can mostly stay at the edge of the core area. But we must also consider people who are less mobile and sometimes depend on a car. The core is being developed near a public transport hub. Public transport will therefore be used the most.

127717.explanation: Task of the province. But together with all involved parties, including the province and landowners, there should be more effort on information provision.

127663.explanation: We advocate for a corrective referendum. 'Participation' evenings are not enough!

127666.explanation: We do not see that there is a problem with opening hours. Some regulation is allowed. Besides hospitality venues, there are also residents who have to get up on time.

127668.explanation: Refugees will not disappear. Wars will continue for the time being. Every municipality must contribute to accommodating refugees.

127669.explanation: Amersfoort shows how it can be done. It is not healthy for people and nature. Exceptions are possible in emergency situations.

127670.explanation: A vibrant center is also nice. A cold center is not attractive. We are in favor of sustainable heating (the municipality can possibly help entrepreneurs with this).

127673.explanation: Agreed for medical reasons. Hearing damage is a serious problem. This applies to both partygoers and local residents.

127674.explanation: That should mainly be for small entrepreneurs who do not have enough budget/time to do this themselves. In addition, control of this can be stricter and more systematic for all properties to which these rules apply.

127676.explanation: Especially for families with an income up to 150 percent of the social minimum! Creativity and culture must be available and remain available to everyone.

127680.explanation: Extra camera surveillance can be installed where necessary.

127682.explanation: We leave room to discuss this if the enforcement officers themselves indicate a need for other means of pressure.

127683.explanation: It is important to prioritize people with a social and economic connection.

127685.explanation: This already applies in Zeist. However, we see that enforcement there needs to be stricter.

127687.explanation: At the same time, develop plans for permanent buildings.

127688.explanation: Let the municipality support people with this if they wish and are able.

127689.explanation: The municipality should not be responsible for people getting further into debt. Let the municipality proactively help people with financial problems.

127694.explanation: Provided that residents are also involved in this.

127695.explanation: Agreed, to protect the environment and promote a healthy living environment.

127696.explanation: We will not start on this.

127698.explanation: Zeist is among the 20 municipalities with the lowest property tax. There is room to increase it somewhat.

127702.explanation: We hear from independent local entrepreneurs that there is no great need to participate in a 24-hour economy. Agreements about opening hours provide peace of mind.

127703.explanation: The municipality must have the option to assess the necessity on a case-by-case basis. This also gives status holders the opportunity to integrate more quickly.

127706.explanation: The Netherlands is a swimming country. It is important for the safety of our children that everyone learns to swim; this also applies to cycling and safe traffic participation.

127707.explanation: We are in favor of a safe and open soft drug policy. More options for consumers. Our municipality has a wide choice of restaurants and cafés, so why just one coffeeshop?

127709.explanation: Tall apartment buildings do not contribute to a pleasant/livable living environment. Flat residents meet each other much less than in a low-rise neighborhood.

127710.explanation: Promoting contact between residents of residential areas benefits livability and combats loneliness.

127712.explanation: We are for a car-restricted center where cars are banned but the center remains accessible to everyone.

127713.explanation: We do not think the KNVB must remain in Zeist at all costs.

127714.explanation: At the moment, there is no possibility for the clinic to move. If we as a municipality decide that clinic Fivoor can no longer remain in our municipality, the consequence is that many of these patients will end up 'on the streets'.

127716.explanation: As long as consideration is given to the accessibility of the core for every resident and visitor. Space must be reserved for a limited number of parking spaces.

127717.explanation: Living with wolves is new for all of the Netherlands. We focus on education and active safety (through tagging and relevant warning messages).

127663.explanation: We support participation but not referendums. Referendums make complex choices black-and-white and increase polarization. Therefore, Volt prefers citizens' assemblies, where randomly selected residents study issues and jointly develop supported solutions. Citizens' assemblies lead to better decisions and more connection.

127666.explanation: A vibrant city is alive in the evening and night. That includes space for hospitality and nightlife culture. Volt wants to give entrepreneurs room to improve nightlife. At the same time, we take nuisance seriously and make clear agreements with neighbors.

127668.explanation: Distribution and solidarity are necessary and we therefore believe we must continue to accommodate people in need in Zeist; even after 2028. Volt believes that asylum seekers who feel safe, learn the language, and work are an added value to our society.

127669.explanation: Open fireplaces and wood stoves cause a lot of fine particles and health complaints. Therefore, Volt opts for discouragement and good information. With the help of the 'Stookwijzer' we clarify when burning is unwise. On days with poor air quality, wood burning can also be temporarily restricted.

127670.explanation: Fossil-fueled terrace heaters must stop because that is pure waste. A warm terrace must not come at the expense of our air and the climate. But we are open to efficient electric solutions.

127673.explanation: Festivals and parties belong to Zeist and enliven our village. Too strict noise standards make organizing them unnecessarily difficult, but that does not mean everything should be allowed. With clear agreements and enforcement, we maintain the balance between fun and livability.

127674.explanation: For entrepreneurs, adjustments are sometimes costly, even though they are socially necessary. That is why we want the municipality to help with this. This makes accessibility feasible and ensures everyone can participate.

127676.explanation: We believe every child should have the opportunity to make music, play sports, or do something creative. That is why Volt wants to make annual funding available for each child up to 18 years old for leisure activities, such as music lessons. This ensures every talent has a fair foundation and chance.

127680.explanation: Cameras increase the chance of catching offenders and contribute to a feeling of safety, though they do not prevent everything. Therefore, we want to deploy cameras targeted at places where they are truly needed, as a complement to prevention and enforcement.

127682.explanation: We want boa’s to do their work safely. However, arming them does not automatically fit their role as approachable enforcers in the neighborhood because it can increase the chance of escalation. Therefore, Volt believes baton use should depend on situations, timing, and locations.

127683.explanation: We find it important that people can live close to their family, friends, and neighborhood. Local networks provide care, support, and cohesion in neighborhoods. By giving priority to residents of Zeist, we keep Zeist strong and ensure people can continue to participate here.

127685.explanation: Volt believes homes are meant for living in, not for making money. A mandatory owner-occupancy rule prevents homes from being bought up for profit while first-time buyers have no chance. This keeps homes available for people who genuinely want to live here.

127687.explanation: Volt wants permanent, affordable housing for students and one-person households. Temporary housing is not a real solution to their housing shortage. It just postpones the problem and creates uncertainty. For temporary quick options, Volt in Zeist looks at promoting landlord rental arrangements.

127688.explanation: Participation is important, but not through coercion or mandatory counter-performances. We prefer an 'activating social assistance': with room to work, learn, or do something for society, without people being cut or stuck in rules. All based on trust and perspective.

127689.explanation: Debt forgiveness prevents small amounts from turning into large debts and provides peace and clarity. Problem debts also cost the government an enormous amount of money. Therefore, we want to actively tackle problematic debts, for example by buying them and paying them off.

127694.explanation: The parking solution needed in a neighborhood depends on the specific parking situation there and must first be investigated together with all involved parties. Introducing a parking permit can otherwise lead to a waterbed effect regarding the parking problem you thought you solved.

127695.explanation: The center must be future-oriented, including for traffic, but without the polluting types. We aim to make the shopping center accessible to visitors and entrepreneurs in a sustainable and healthy way. Good consultation with all involved entrepreneurs is needed about the 'how' regarding center freight traffic.

127696.explanation: Volt believes good care must be equally accessible to everyone and that the patient’s background must be taken into account. But 'always being able to choose' is not realistic. Care providers and patients must therefore treat each other with respect.

127698.explanation: Of course, many people watch their housing costs, but the property tax (ozb) in Zeist is relatively low, and lowering it means less money for care, safety, green spaces, and facilities in neighborhoods. Volt therefore chooses to invest in the future, the welfare of Zeist residents, and financially healthy accounting.

127702.explanation: We believe entrepreneurs should be able to decide for themselves when they are open. Opening hours give shops, hospitality, and visitors flexibility to organize their time. That suits a lively and accessible shopping center.

127703.explanation: We believe that people who are allowed to stay should be able to build their lives as quickly as possible. A home ensures faster integration and more peace to effectively pick up life. But the latter applies to many housing seekers and allocation based on urgency must therefore be broadly careful.

127706.explanation: We believe every child must be able to grow up safely in our water-rich country. School swimming increases swimming skills and reduces risks. By investing in this, we ensure safety and equal opportunities.

127707.explanation: A realistic approach to soft drugs is desirable. Coffeeshops offer fixed locations, clear rules, and supervision regarding the sale of weed/hash. That is safe for users and the environment around the coffeeshop. But! Zeist currently has one coffeeshop in the center; a second seems unnecessary given Zeist’s size.

127709.explanation: Zeist's green character requires careful choices: high-rise buildings may be realized in the right place and with the right quality. By also looking at innovative vertical greenery, we connect residential quality with climate adaptation and preserve the wooded character.

127710.explanation: We find strong neighborhood centers essential for a healthy town. Neighborhood centers are places where people meet, find help, and organize activities together. They strengthen local networks and prevent loneliness and problems.

127712.explanation: Volt wants to restore the regional function of Zeist’s shopping center. Instead of making it car-free, we focus on reducing car traffic by offering visitors good alternatives, including a tram. This way we think along with entrepreneurs and keep the center livable, sustainable, and accessible.

127713.explanation: The KNVB wants to remain in Zeist due to historic roots and strategic advantages but faces the challenge of expanding the campus to remain future-proof. Because of the KNVB's large social role, Volt hopes the KNVB will stay in Zeist, but not at any cost.

127714.explanation: Fivoor must leave Zeist because this has explicitly already been promised and the emotional stress around the fatal incidents is too great. Reversing the announced departure now would make the municipality lose credibility. This does not affect the fact that such care facilities are needed somewhere.

127716.explanation: The new core is located at a high-quality public transport stop, so car demand there can be small. However, the car demand of the surroundings, which also use the public transport, does not simply change without good alternatives. A tram can provide such an alternative but will not make the core 100% car-free, only less car-dependent.

127717.explanation: Volt advocates a balanced approach: protection according to European legislation combined with a clear provincial culling policy for problem wolves and possibly tracking for more knowledge about the wolf, along with support for animal owners, such as donkey shelters, in preventing wolves.

127663.explanation: Yes, through an advisory multiple-choice referendum. Do not reduce complex questions to a simple for/against choice. Apply it to issues of substantial social importance and ensure that the voices of nature, animals, and future generations are heard.

127666.explanation: They can already do this to a large extent. Extending opening hours into very late or night hours quickly causes nuisance for local residents and others. It also has many disadvantages for the (health of) staff due to high workload, fatigue, and long and irregular working hours.

127668.explanation: Asylum reception is important and can also take place spread over more locations in the municipality. The agreement with Utrechts Landschap is that the current reception site at Kamp van Zeist will be converted into nature. Smaller reception locations often also offer quicker contact with the environment (better integration).

127669.explanation: Open fireplaces and wood stoves cause harmful emissions and nuisance.

127670.explanation: Terrace heating wastes a lot of energy, emits harmful substances, contributes to air pollution, and is expensive. Moreover, it is often not effective against the cold. Blankets and warm clothing are more suitable against the cold and do not have all these disadvantages of terrace heating.

127673.explanation: It is important for young people and others to enjoy festivals and parties. The sound does not need to be so loud that it causes noise nuisance and damage to local residents and others.

127674.explanation: It is important that entrepreneurs make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities. It is not the municipality's task to finance this. The municipality also does not have sufficient financial means for that.

127676.explanation: Music lessons can play an important role in the development of children. Every child deserves to be able to take music lessons, whether a family is poor or rich.

127680.explanation: Use camera surveillance temporarily and only for risk areas. Do not apply camera surveillance generally and outside risk areas, as that does not fit an open, free, and democratic society.

127682.explanation: More important than a baton is that enforcement officers (boa's) can resolve nuisance and scuffles without using violence. In some cases, a baton may be necessary but not as standard equipment.

127683.explanation: Affordable housing is important for many housing seekers. When municipalities exclude housing seekers from other municipalities, it obstructs the free choice of people about where they want to live.

127685.explanation: Housing is a scarce and valuable good. Living is a basic need. Houses are meant to live in, not to make money from.

127687.explanation: The housing shortage among students is great and every person deserves a place to live. Temporary housing such as container homes can relieve the housing shortage and are relocatable to places in the built environment where space is available at that moment.

127688.explanation: Performing socially meaningful work can be in the interest of society and the individual involved. But imposing it with coercion is not desirable.

127689.explanation: Residents and families with a low income often struggle to make ends meet and to provide for their general daily needs. Waiving municipal taxes is important for such residents and families.

127694.explanation: Parking permits reduce parking pressure and allow residents to park in their living environment. It provides more space for greenery, trees, and playgrounds for children. And it stimulates the use of (shared) bicycles. Do it in consultation with residents. Also promote car-free neighborhoods.

127695.explanation: Polluting vehicles cause harmful emissions and damage to the health of residents and visitors.

127696.explanation: The quality of care does not depend on the faith, culture, or beliefs of the care provider.

127698.explanation: This is already very low in Zeist; lowering it further risks the municipality being cut in its contribution from the national government. Moreover, revenues from property tax enable the municipality to finance important social tasks.

127702.explanation: Sunday afternoon opening hours are sufficient. Longer Sunday opening hours disadvantage smaller shops that cannot be open on Sundays. It is also disadvantageous for staff and employees who are forced to work on Sundays for longer hours.

127706.explanation: Every year in water-rich Netherlands, children and sometimes adults drown again. This causes much suffering. School swimming helps to prevent drowning.

127707.explanation: Regular coffeeshops are safer for users than the illegal circuit and cause much less nuisance and police involvement. Where there is a demand for such products, it is better to regulate their sale so that there is supervision over the sales and the composition of the products.

127709.explanation: High apartment blocks are not a goal in themselves. When faced with the choice between building in forest or nature versus building upwards, it is important to protect the forest and nature.

127710.explanation: Village centers can play an important connecting role for residents. It is important, however, that these village centers are actively used and supported by the village residents.

127712.explanation: This is not always possible on main roads in the center, but it is important to strive for a low-car city center where feasible. A car-free center improves health and quality of life for residents and visitors.

127713.explanation: The KNVB is welcome to stay, but not if expansion comes at the expense of Zeist forest and protected nature. Let alone at the cost of hectares of Zeist forest.

127714.explanation: Forensic clinics are important for treating patients and also for the safety of residents. There are few suitable locations for Fivoor in Zeist. Another location within the municipality is not immediately obvious, but it is not excluded.

127716.explanation: The core area is located next to Driebergen-Zeist station and near the bus station. This makes the homes especially suitable for people who want to travel by public transport rather than by car.

127717.explanation: The municipality must primarily invest more money in jointly designing and (having) executing a wolf plan with experts, residents, nature organizations, and others, with attention to both humans and animals.

statements.title: Zeist should spend more money on protecting people and animals from the wolf.
The residents of Zeist should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Zeist moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: Zeist should introduce a parking permit in more neighborhoods.

titleParts.text: Zeist moet in meer wijken een

titleParts.glossary: A permit to park in a certain place. You have to pay for that right.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Zeist.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality must invest in temporary housing for students, for example container housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld steken in tijdelijke huisvesting voor studenten, bijvoorbeeld

titleParts.glossary: A movable and temporary dwelling, usually made from a shipping container.

statements.title: The asylum reception in our municipality should close in 2028 as agreed.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zoals afgesproken in 2028 sluiten.

statements.title: Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: In Zeist the use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves should remain permitted.

titleParts.text: In Zeist moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: Zeist should allow more high-rise residential buildings.
Zeist should try to keep the football association KNVB within the municipal boundaries.

titleParts.glossary: Royal Dutch Football Association, the national football organization.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality must ban outdoor patio heaters.
The BOAs who enforce safety in Zeist should be given a baton.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Zeist controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: Anyone who buys a home in our municipality must live in it themselves.
All villages in the municipality must have their own village center.
Zeist should introduce a low-emission zone to keep polluting vehicles out of the center.

titleParts.glossary: An area where very polluting cars are not allowed to drive.

titleParts.text: invoeren om vervuilende voertuigen uit het centrum te weren.

statements.title: The forensic clinic Fivoor in Den Dolder should be relocated to another site within the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Forensic means judicial. Investigation carried out to collect evidence, for example in a crime.

titleParts.text: kliniek Fivoor in Den Dolder moet een andere plek binnen de gemeente krijgen.

statements.title: When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent for people with a low income. In 2026 that is at most €932.93 per month.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent for people with a low income. In 2026 that is at most €932.93 per month.

statements.title: The municipality should allow longer shop opening hours on Sundays.
The center of Zeist should become car-free.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of a new coffeeshop (cannabis coffee shop).

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.theme: Closing times for bars and restaurants
statements.title: Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
The new district to be built at Driebergen-Zeist station should be car-free.

titleParts.glossary: A village or small town that is part of the municipality.

titleParts.text: die moet komen bij station Driebergen-Zeist moet autovrij worden.
